title: centaurea maculosa subsp. albida
intro: Centaurea maculosa subsp. albida, also known as spotted knapweed, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Centaurea maculosa subsp. albida include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat various health issues, such as skin infections, digestive problems, and respiratory issues.
